articles.summary: AI for the sake of AI gets your team nowhere—especially when you're already lost in email threads, random updates, and a sea of spreadsheets.

articles.title: a leader in work & project management. Try Asana for free >>
THE SLOP INVASION
articles.summary: Low-cost AI ads now overwhelm social feeds and reshape digital advertising around speed and volume instead of brand quality. Performance marketers use AI to produce and test hundreds or thousands of ad variations in minutes and for cents. This rewards strange visuals and cheap clicks over coherence. This strategy benefits dropshippers and fast fashion firms but degrades user experience and erodes trust in ads. Meta is struggling to control the flood since Chinese advertisers spend billions on it, and most ads do not violate clear rules. Human creative work is losing value as automation outperforms it on cost and scale.

articles.title: VOTERS SEE A MIDDLE-CLASS LIFESTYLE AS DRIFTING OUT OF REACH
articles.summary: Most Americans feel a middle-class life is slipping out of reach. This is driven by rising costs for housing, education, health care, and retirement. While many can still cover basics, nearly 60% worry about affording essentials. Only 14% feel they are getting ahead financially. Younger adults feel the strain most. Many say they are worse off than their parents, while older Americans report far less stress. The result is widespread economic pessimism, even as consumer spending holds up and markets remain strong.

articles.title: WIN REPORT: SIX WORDS, SEVEN PERCENT MORE LEADS
articles.summary: Adding six risk reduction words and a proof element increased sign-ups for Mobbin's free plan by 7%. The test targeted hidden objections about free trials, such as fear of credit card requirements and difficulty canceling. The variation added the line “No credit card required. Cancel anytime.” before the call to action and replaced client logos with a claim of supporting 870,000+ designers worldwide. These changes reduced perceived risk at the moment of commitment and improved conversion even though the offer already looked strong.

articles.title: THE PROFIT-TO-ACQUISITION RATIO: HOW TO SET REALISTIC TARGETS WHEN STAKEHOLDERS HAVE UNREALISTIC EXPECTATIONS
articles.summary: The Profit-to-Acquisition Ratio helps set realistic targets based on real unit economics and clear tradeoffs. It starts by calculating breakeven ROAS or CPA from profit margin. The stakeholder or client can then choose how much profit to reinvest, which is a business decision more than a PPC decision. A 50% profit reinvestment is a good starting place for balanced growth.

articles.title: CLAUDE AND FIGJAM
articles.summary: Teams can now turn AI conversations with Claude into editable FigJam diagrams from text, PDFs, or images. This makes it easier to visualize user journeys and project timelines while reducing back-and-forth. Diagrams become shared artifacts that help teams align, spot issues early, and refine ideas together.

articles.title: HOW TO TRACK AI OVERVIEWS: MENTIONS, CITATIONS, CLICK LOSS, AND THE TRAFFIC GOOGLE WON'T SHOW YOU
articles.summary: AI Overviews are hard to measure because Google hides direct data. Performance must be inferred through citations, keyword ownership, brand mentions, and indirect traffic signals. Teams can estimate click loss and visibility shifts by tracking where pages appear, spotting new and missed AIO keywords, and analyzing CTR drops without ranking changes. Combining Search Console workarounds with tools or lightweight trackers helps reveal which content still drives ROI.

articles.title: BETTER MARKET INTELLIGENCE USING AI
articles.summary: AI can mislead market intelligence by inventing facts and using outdated information. The right approach is to treat AI as a grounded research assistant that works only from trusted sources and ties every claim to evidence you can inspect. Better results come from precise prompts that require sources and explicit rules against guessing. Strong workflows verify key claims and avoid decisions based on a single source.

articles.title: NEW FRAMEWORK ON REVENUE MOTIONS
articles.summary: Most companies confuse GTM tactics with growth strategy. Inbound or outbound explain how leads arrive, not how revenue flows. Early growth works by accident, but scale exposes the gaps as products and teams multiply. Revenue motions solve this by intentionally connecting a specific customer, product, GTM approach, and team ownership. Funnels assume one buyer path, which breaks in modern B2B. Without clear revenue motions, activity rises while efficiency falls. Defining them helps leaders drive intentional growth.

articles.title: WEBFLOW MARKETING STRATEGY: HOW WEBFLOW BUILT THE MOST POWERFUL NO-CODE WEBSITE BUILDER
articles.summary: Webflow owned the middle ground between simple site builders and full custom development. It gave non-coders real control without developer dependency. It targeted professional designers, agencies, and fast-moving marketing teams by using technical language made visual, which reinforced a sense of craft and pricing power. Growth came from education-driven content like Webflow University, a community ecosystem of 85,000+ members, and agency-led distribution that turned partners into a scalable sales force.

articles.title: REMARKETING DOESN'T MAKE SENSE ANYMORE
articles.summary: Meta already prioritizes high-intent users automatically, making isolated remarketing audiences mostly redundant.

articles.title: THE “PINTEREST PANTRY” VS. REALITY
articles.summary: Storage trends dominating social include “The Sunday Reset Phenomenon", fridgescaping, and the side hustle stockroom.

articles.summary: Lessons about virality, fandoms, and inclusion.
